A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN THE TOWN OF ROME...ALONG LAMPHEAR ROAD. A MANUFACTURED HOME WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED. SOME TREE...TELEPHONE POLE...AND CROP DAMAGE WAS ALSO OBSERVED IN THE VICNITY. MAXIMUM TORNADO WINDS OF EF-1 INTENSITY WERE ESTIMATED...BETWEEN 100 AND 110 MPH.

TORNADO TOUCHDOWN NEAR INTERSECTION OF RYERS CREEK ROAD AND RIVER ROAD. TREE DAMAGE...WITH ONE TREE FALLING ON A HOUSE. TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE 100 YARDS WIDE AND TRAVELED 0.3 MILES. MAXIMUM SPEEDS OF 80-90 MPH...RATED EF-1
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON WELCH ROAD IN CORNING. THERE WERE MANY TREE SNAPS AND UPROOTS. FOR THE NEXT 3 TO 4 MILES...THE TORNADO LIFTED AT TIMES. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG BROWN HOLLOW ROAD. A SWATH OF DAMAGE...INCLUDING APPROXIMATELY 200-300 TREES...WAS SEEN. WINDS REACHED 100-110 MPH. THE DAMAGE PATH APPEARED TO END JUST WEST OF MURPHY ROAD...STILL IN SOUTH CORNING. THE TORNADO WAS ABOUT 800 YARDS WIDE AND TRAVELED 6.5 MILES. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE BETWEEN 90-110 MPH. RATED EF-1.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. MANY ON HOUSES.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AROUND CORNING COMMUNITY COLLEGE.

TORNADO TOUCH DOWN NEAR SOUTH CORNING CAUSING DAMAGE TO TREES AND HOMES. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURED ALONG WITH SIDING AND FLASHING DAMAGE. DEBRIS PENETRATED SOME HOUSES. TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE 200 YARDS WIDE AND TRAVELED FOR 2.75 MILES.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN... NUMEROUS ROOFS OFF BUILDINGS... PEOPLE TRAPPED IN VEHICLES
FOLLOW UP TO PSBL TORNADO IN ELMIRA - DAMAGE AT ARNOT MALL TO WHERE MACYS WAS TAKING ON WATER THROUGH DAMAGED ROOF AND THERE WAS DAMAGE AT THE ELMIRA COUNTRY CLUB

CONFIRMED LONGTRACK TORNADO FROM NORTH CHEMUNG TO JUST NORTH OF BARTON IN CHEMUNG AND TIOGA COUNTIES NEW YORK. THIS IS A NEW TOUCHDOWN THAT PRODUCED TORNADOES IN SOUTH CORNING AND ELMIRA. CLASSIFIED AS AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH A WIND OF BETWEEN 100-110 MPH. MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE WAS TO SOFTWOOD AND HARDWOOD TREES WHICH WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. ALSO... A FEW HOMES... GAARAGES AND SHEDS WERE DAMAGED BY WIND WITH TRIM OR SIDING TORN OR SHINGLES OFF ROOFS. SOME HOMES HAD MORE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE AS
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. ALSO SMALL HAIL
